title: Review Google Business Profile Failed Appeal and Verification Path
support: Marketing support after Andrew approves source-account scope
due: Immediate / before relying on public Google profile
tool: Google Business Profile source account + Gmail/source evidence
deliverable: Failed appeal and unverified-profile state are reviewed in the source account; video verification or correction path is approved; evidence is preserved; no unsupported public-profile edit is made.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Marketing & Brand / Administration
context: Google says the appeal was not approved, the Business Profile is not verified, and further verification is required before edits can be visible. This is the most urgent public-facing Hub item from the July 22 Cockpit read.
nextSteps: Review the Business Profile source account and capture the exact failed-appeal or verification issue.
Decide whether video verification, correction materials, or another approved path is feasible.
Draft a fact-only evidence packet for Andrew review if support is delegated.
Do not edit the profile, use verification codes, submit an appeal, or rely on the public profile until Andrew approves the path.
Log the source-backed outcome or blocker in the Cockpit.
requiredEvidence: Source-account status note or screenshot summary showing failed appeal, verification requirement, approved next path, and any blocker.
verification1: Andrew/Admin verifies the Business Profile source-account status.
verification2: Andrew confirms the verification, appeal, correction, or park decision.
cockpitBacklink: Action Tracker task-google-business-profile-suspension-appeal; Decision Log 2026-07-21 Google Business Profile row; Source Inbox rows 447-448
prompt: In DSG GPT, draft a Google Business Profile failed-appeal evidence packet. Include source-account status, exact issue, video-verification feasibility, correction path, no-action guardrails, owner/support/approver, required evidence, Verification 1, Verification 2, and Cockpit backlink.

title: Decide CitizensNYC / Vanguard Voices Funding Path
support: Clark / Richard / grant helper after Andrew confirms scope
approves: Andrew; Board for restricted funding, public claims, or material commitments
due: Final draft target July 24; CitizensNYC deadline July 27
tool: July 20 funding report + CitizensNYC thread + Grants Folder
deliverable: Andrew decides apply/park, project scope, budget lane, Brooklyn route, organization-level identity language, and review path; helpers prepare only review-first materials until approved.
context: The July 20 report makes CitizensNYC the act-this-week grant. Richard confirmed the Vanguard assumptions, so the live blocker is Andrew/Clark final scope, budget, and approval boundaries.
nextSteps: Confirm apply or park for CitizensNYC.
Set final project scope, simple budget, Brooklyn footprint, and organization-level identity language.
Decide whether Clark or all-eyes review is needed before submission planning.
Name any grant/admin helper and boundaries for packet preparation only.
Do not submit, contact funders, create accounts, share banking information, disclose identity details, or promise sponsor benefits without approval.
requiredEvidence: Funding decision note naming apply/park, scope, budget lane, approved language, reviewer path, and no-external-action status.
verification1: Andrew verifies the funding path and packet scope.
verification2: Andrew/Clark or board confirms external-use approval or keeps the item review-first.
cockpitBacklink: Action Tracker task-funding-readiness-staging; Decision Log 2026-07-20 funding priorities row; July 20 funding report
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a CitizensNYC/Vanguard Voices review packet. Include apply/park decision, scope, simple budget, Brooklyn footprint, identity-language boundary, Clark/all-eyes review question, no-external-action guardrails, required evidence, Verification 1, Verification 2, and Cockpit backlink.

title: Monitor Google for Nonprofits / Workspace Re-Review

Document approval or exact remaining gaps.

verification2: Andrew approves closure or the next website/account/resubmission action.

nextSteps: Confirm which assignments are confirmed versus Proposed / Needs Alignment.
Separate owner, support, reviewer, and final approver wherever possible.
State priority in words rather than relying on colors.
